sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of tree tomatoes grown from Cyphomandra betacea Sendt or Solanum betaceum Cav. of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Tree Tomatoes for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of tree tomatoes grown from Cyphomandra betacea Sendt or Solanum betaceum Cav. of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Tree Tomatoes for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of tree tomatoes grown from Cyphomandra betacea Sendt or Solanum betaceum Cav. of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Tree Tomatoes for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
number=2
level=1

sections.title: Minimum Requirements
sections.text: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the tree tomatoes must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean and practically free of any visible foreign matter; practically free of pests and damage caused by them affecting the general appearance of the produce; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste; firm; fresh in appearance; with the stalk present to the first knot.

level=2

page_spans.text: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the tree tomatoes must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean and practically free of any visible foreign matter; practically free of pests and damage caused by them affecting the general appearance of the produce; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste; firm; fresh in appearance; with the stalk present to the first knot.

sections.title: The tree tomatoes must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness 2 account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The tree tomatoes must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness 2 account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and the area in which they are grown.
sections.text: The development and condition of the tree tomatoes must be such as to enable them: to withstand transport and handling; and to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Tree tomatoes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > “Extra” Class
number=2.2.1
level=3
Tree tomatoes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Tree tomatoes in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Tree tomatoes in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: slight defects in shape; slight defects of the skin such as scratches and blemishes, not exceeding more than 10% of the total surface area of the fruit. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

page_spans.text: Tree tomatoes in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: slight defects in shape; slight defects of the skin such as scratches and blemishes, not exceeding more than 10% of the total surface area of the fruit. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes tree tomatoes which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirement s specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the tree tomatoes retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: defects in shape, such as extension or flattening of the apex; defects in colouring and of the skin such as scratches and blemishes, not exceeding 20% of the total surface area of the fruit. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Tree tomatoes may be sized by diameter, weight or count.

number=3
level=1

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
number=4
level=1
Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

page_spans.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only tree tomatoes of the same origin, variety, quality, colour and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Tree tomatoes must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new 3, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Tree tomatoes shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: 7.1 The produce covered by this  Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).  7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

level=1

page_spans.text: 7.1 The produce covered by this  Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).  7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables  (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.  8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the tree tomatoes. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sentences.original: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of tree tomatoes grown from Cyphomandra betacea Sendt or Solanum betaceum Cav. of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

classification.sentence: Tree Tomatoes for industrial processing are excluded.
classification.pattern: Declarative exclusion statement
classification.justification: Sentence states an exclusion from the standard scope, a typical scope condition phrased as a negation/exclusion sentence, which does not strongly map to any known template.
notes: Exclusion clauses within scope are common but structurally distinct from prescriptive or obligation sentences.

sentences.original: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the tree tomatoes must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean and practically free of any visible foreign matter; practically free of pests and damage caused by them affecting the general appearance of the produce; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste; firm; fresh in appearance; with the stalk present to the first knot.

classification.sentence: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the tree tomatoes must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean and practically free of any visible foreign matter; practically free of pests and damage caused by them affecting the general appearance of the produce; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste; firm; fresh in appearance; with the stalk present to the first knot.
classification.pattern: Complex obligation sentence with enumerated quality requirements and embedded exclusion clause

components.scope_condition: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed
obligations: sound (produce affected by rotting or deterioration making it unfit is excluded)
clean and practically free of visible foreign matter
practically free of pests and related damage affecting appearance
free of abnormal external moisture except condensation after cold storage
free of any foreign smell and/or taste
with stalk present to first knot

classification.justification: Complex normative statement listing multiple obligation criteria with conditional qualification and exclusion. No single known template matches entire enumerated structure.
notes: Combination of obligations and embedded exclusions creates a new pattern.

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

sentences.original: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables  (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.  8.2

classification.sentence: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (should) + verb (comply) + object (criteria) + relative clause specifying criteria source

components.subject: The produce
components.criteria_source: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by requiring compliance with established microbiological criteria. Its structure features a subject followed by a modal verb and verb phrase describing compliance with specific criteria referenced by a guideline. No exact or close template from the catalog matches this specific compliance citation structure, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The modal 'should' here conveys an obligation in normative regulatory context.
The sentence references an external standard for criteria but does not specify numerical limits, hence falls under obligation rather than limits and targets.

sentences.original: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

pages.title: STANDARD FOR TREE TOMATOES
pages.summary: A página contém o título do documento, um cabeçalho com código e data, e uma única seção com título numerado '1' e texto associado vazio. A estrutura segue corretamente o padrão RAG para uma página inicial.
Página com título numerado principal e subseções estruturadas numericamente até o nível 3, com texto explicativo associado. O conteúdo da seção 2.2.3 continua na próxima página.

A página contém seções numeradas e estruturadas até o nível 3, com títulos claros e texto explicativo associado. Não há indicações de continuidade de conteúdo de pagina anterior ou para a próxima página.
A página contém seções numeradas do nível 1 ao 3, com títulos claros e texto associado para usos RAG. Não há continuidade entre páginas. O cabeçalho 'CXS 303-2011 4' foi identificado.
